project.name: Briefly
project.goal: A web app that turns a team's weekly activity into a two-paragraph stakeholder brief.
project.primary_user: An engineering manager who writes the same status update every Friday.
project.core_problem: Weekly stakeholder updates are assembled by hand from tickets, PRs, and chat; the writing is repetitive and the gathering is worse.
project.core_journey:
1. The manager connects the team's issue tracker export.
2. The app drafts a two-paragraph brief with linked evidence for every claim.
3. The manager edits inline and copies the approved brief out.
project.success_measure: A manager produces an approved, evidence-linked brief in under five minutes, down from thirty.
project.success_metrics:
- time to approved brief -- under 5 minutes
- claims with a working evidence link -- 100%
project.riskiest_assumption: An LLM can draft claims that are faithful to the underlying tickets often enough that editing beats writing from scratch.
project.req_ac_list:
- [ ] **REQ-AC1:** Importing a week's export yields a draft brief with every claim linked to at least one source item.
- [ ] **REQ-AC2:** Editing a paragraph and approving produces a copyable final brief.
- [ ] **REQ-AC3:** A claim with no supporting source is visually flagged, never silently kept.
project.non_goals:
- No live integrations in the first iteration; file export import only.
- No scheduling or sending; the manager copies the brief out.
project.constraint_time: Six weeks to an internal pilot with two teams.
project.constraint_cost: LLM budget USD 50/month for the pilot; one draft must cost under USD 0.10.
project.constraint_data: Ticket exports may contain internal names; data stays in the company tenancy and drafts are deleted after 30 days.
project.deployment_target: Internal web app on the company PaaS.
project.scale_expectations: Two pilot teams, about 20 briefs per week.
project.integrations:
- Issue-tracker CSV/JSON export (file import).
- Anthropic API (drafting and faithfulness evals).
project.in_scope_list:
- Export import and per-claim evidence linking.
- LLM-drafted two-paragraph brief with inline editing.
- Faithfulness flagging for unsupported claims.
project.pain_point: writes the same status update every Friday
project.current_alternative: hand-assembling updates from tickets, PRs, and chat
project.key_benefit: drafts the brief with receipts, so editing replaces writing
project.key_differentiator: every claim carries a link to its evidence, and unsupported claims are flagged

positive_reference.ref: the clean SPA structure of excalidraw/excalidraw
